PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION, APPOINTMENT UNDER IAEA
Off Calendar Jun-22-2016 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Avid Alavi for petitioner Thomas Lucas; Marta Eisenberg for Mara Moreira.

Ms. Alavi reported that there are no objections, and that Ms. Moreira has voluntarily resigned as personal representative. She stated that the resignation has been filed with the Court. The Court inquired regarding the new bond amount. Ms. Alavi stated that taxes for the estate have been paid, reducing the amount of the funds to be administered. She requested that the amunt of the new bond be set as $279,000.00. The Court granted the petition, confirming the figure for the new bond to be $279,000.00 (subject to review of the order).

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Craig Blackstone. Bailiff: Deputy Barrientos. (Not reported)

PROBATE ACCOUNTING STATUS PETITION
Off Calendar May-18-2016 Continued To Oct-03-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Avid Alavi for Thomas Lucas (Petitioner). Marta Eisenberg for Mara Moreira (Administrator)

Ms. Eisenberg stated that Mara is residing in Germany where she is taking care of the decedent two children, that they are working to reach a settlement and requested a five month continuance and to approve the settlement agreement on file.

Ms. Alavi confirmed that she has received a proposed settlement, that the goal is for Mara to get a loan to repay the estate, that a neuropsych is prepared to abate taxes, that there are 2 parcels of real property that can be refinanced, that she is in agreement to the requested five month continuance and requested a 30 day continuance for the petition of letters of administration to give time to serve Mara now that they know where she is.

The Petition for letters of administration continued to June 20, 2016 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The Petition for surcharge of former administrator etc., the status on accounting, the status for filing a petition for final distribution were continued to October 3, 2016 at 9:00 a.m. in Dept. 204. A status of filing a petition for approval of settlement agreement was set for October 3, 2016 at 9:00 a.m. in Dept. 204.

Judge Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Nancy Wong/ Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Barrientos. Not Reported.

PROBATE ACCOUNTING STATUS PETITION
Off Calendar Sep-15-2015 Continued To Jan-19-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Avid Alavi for (special administrator) Thomas Lucas.

Ms. Alavi reported that the accounting is done but there are problems, that there was a withdrawal of $100,000.00, that it is not in the guardianship account, that Mr. Blacksburg does not know where it went, that Mr. Der suggests the possibility that another account was opened and that they are trying to track this, that the former administrator had a CPA do the taxes for 2011, that $400,000.00 to $500,000.00 was due but never paid and has increased to $637,434.00. Ms. Alavi further reported that they do not know where the former administrator is, possibly in Germany, that she owns property in San Francisco, that the 2012 & 2013 taxes were extended but not filed, that they will file an extension for 2014, that it may be some time before they can close and requested a 120 day continuance, that the status be taken off calendar and that Mr. Lucas be appointed as administrator of the estate. The accounting status report and the petition for letters of administration were continued to January 19, 2016 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Paul H. Alvarado. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Kabasares. (Not Reported)

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION, APPOINTMENT UNDER IAEA
Off Calendar Nov-10-2014 Continued To Apr-27-15 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: The following cases were heard together: PES-10-294005 Estate of Michael Kanaley & PGN-10-294051 Guardianship of Janaina Moreira-Kanaley & Larissa Kanaley.
Appearances: Avid Alavi for Thomas Lucas (case PES-10-294005 only). Michael Blacksburg for Mara Moreira. Ernest Der as court appointed counsel for (minors) Janaina Moreira-Kanaley & Larissa Kanaley.

Mr. Blacksburg reported that Ms. Moreira is not responding to his communications including the recent examiner's notes that he sent to her. Mr. Der stated that it would be best for Mr. Lucas to remain as special administrator, the estate needs to be wrapped up, that it would be challenging and costly to remove Ms. Moreira as personal representative, that Mr. Lucas could do an accounting and final distribution. Ms. Alavi reported that Mr. Lucas has marshalled the bank accounts, is still working on marshalling the e-trade account and making sure that taxes have been paid. Ms. Alavi requests a 5 month continuance for Mr. Lucas to prepare the accounting. Mr. Der stated that he will continue to try and reach Ms. Moreira, the funds are in blocked accounts, and feels that the estate should be finalized before dealing with the guardianship. The court stated that it has not received anything from the guardian since January 2014, someone needs to be in place and requested that Mr. Der file a petition to remove Ms. Maura as guardian. The matters in the guardianship case, status on filing annual written status report, filing of receipt of distribution in the estate case were continued to January 27, 2015 at 1:30 p.m., in Dept. 204. The petition for removal of guardian is to be set for this date as well. The matter in the estate case, petition for letters of administration, was continued to April 27, 2015 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 and status of filing an accounting is set for that date as well. The court will extend letters of special administration until further order of the court, Ms. Avid submitted a proposed order.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Rosie Noguera/Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy R. McConico. (Not Reported)

EX PARTE PETITION FOR LETTERS OF SPECIAL ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Oct-24-2014 Granted Per Written Order

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status For Closing The Estate
Off Calendar Oct-06-2014 Continued To Dec-08-14 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Michael Blacksburg for (administrator) Mara Moreira. Lawrence Siracusa for (previous special administrator) Thomas Lucas. Deborah Fox on behalf of Ernest Der for (minors) Janaina Kanaley & Larissa Kanaley. Jonathon Ferdon for the San Francisco Public Guardian. Todd Christianson of Phillips Bonding Company also present.

Mr. Blacksburg reported that he closed his business several months ago, Ms. Moreira is not cooperating with finalizing the estate, that Mr. Der has had some success with Ms. Moreira and is asking for Thomas Lucas to be appointed to close the estate. Mr. Ferdon stated that the court requested him to be here, that an accounting by Ms. Moreira must be completed prior to anyone stepping in, there are discrepancies between the amounts in the blocked accounts, the amount bonded and the amount gained in the stock options. Mr. Siracusa stated that Mr. Lucas is agreeable to stepping in, that he would be able to do a forensic accounting, that most of the money is in blocked accounts. Mr. Ferdon stated that the blocked accounts seem to be short $200,000.00, that a forensic accounting would be possible but costly and that an alternative would be to have Mr. Blacksburg do the accounting. Ms. Fox stated that Ms. Moreira is interviewing and seeking counsel to substitute in for Mr. Blacksburg and will prepare the accounting with the assistance of new counsel, but also suggested suspending Ms. Moreira as administrator and appointing Mr. Lucas in that position. Mr. Ferdon stated that other family members have priority over Mr. Lucas and should be contacted. The status on closing the estate was continued to December 8, 2014 at 9:00 a.m.., in Dept. 204. The request for suspension of powers and appointment of successor administrator was taken under submission. The order to appear is taken off calendar.

Judge: Paul H. Alavarado. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Choi. (Not Reported)

Mr. Blacksburg also stated that the Minors are with Ms. Moreira, that he has both her international cell phone number and e mail address, that everything is in blocked accounts except for a couple of thousand dollars, that the amounts are close to $450,000.00 for the Estate and 1.5 million for the Guardianship in six different blocked accounts. Off Calendar Aug-29-2012 Continued To Sep-10-12 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Ernest Der Court appointed Counsel for the Minors.

The Court noted that Mr. Balcksburg was not present. Mr. Der stated that he has been involved in e-mail exchange, that it was a slow transfer of funds from the E-Trade account and the Fidelity account to the blocked account, that from what he has seen the accounts seem to exist and that it is his instinct that they are close but he can't say for sure. ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Jul-31-2012 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Ernest Der as Court Appointed Attorney for the Minors. Michael Blacksburg for and with Mara Moreira (Executor). Lawrence Siracusa for Thomas Lucas (Special Administrator).

Mr. Blacksburg gave a summary of the case and reported that the E-Trade account has over $900,000.00 and that he will increase the bond. They are waiting to have a referee appointed to complete the inventory and appraisal. All parties agreed to approve Mr. Lucas's report. The Court granted Mr. Luca's report on waiver of account and exonerate the bond (subject to review of the order). Discussions were held with regard to the additional bond or to placed the funds into a blocked account. Status of filing of the additional bond and blocked account receipts is set on July 17, 2012 at 9:00 am in Dept. 204. Status of filing inventory and appraisal is continued to September 18, 2012 at 9:00 am in Dept. 204.

Petition For Special Administration
Off Calendar Dec-28-2010 Granted Per Written Order

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Dec-21-2010 Continued To Feb-24-11 At 01:30 P.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Michael Blacksburg for Mara Moreira. Jon Vaught for James Kanaley (Objector).

Mr. Vaught filed a competing petition and an objection this morning. Counsel presented oral argument to support their positions with regard to Ms. Moreira's residency. Counsel agreed to Ms. Moreira serving as Special Administrator with limited authority to exersize stock options with posting of bond. The Examiner will contact Counsel to inform them if an ex parte petition is required for Special Administration. If an ex parte petition is required, Mr. Vaught waives notice. Both probate petitions were continued to February 24, 2011 at 1:30 p.m., in Dept. 204 for two half-day evidentiary hearings on the issue of residency. The second evidentiary hearing is scheduled for February 25, 2011 at 9:30 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy A. Payne.
